mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recessionary recovery phase reflects expanding corporate output capacity and improving profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 5-day SPX returns during post-recessionary periods, with a directional accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of restrictive monetary policy and higher discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, supporting higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative 60-day SPX return in > 55% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly contractions in real GDP reflect shrinking aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate revenue fundamentals, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, with a Rank IC < -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial output during a liquidity-driven stimulus regime indicates strong manufacturing resilience and inventory restocking, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns during stimulus periods, with directional accuracy below 40%.
